questions.question: Which Spring annotation, applied where, triggers Jakarta Bean Validation for a @RequestBody DTO?
choices: @Valid on the @RequestBody method parameter; the DTO's fields are then checked against their Jakarta annotations.
No annotation is required; Jackson validates automatically.
questions.explanation: @Valid on the @RequestBody parameter is the trigger for body validation; it cascades into the DTO's constraint annotations. @Validated on the class is needed separately to enable constraint checks on @RequestParam/@PathVariable values.

questions.question: You add @Min(1) @Max(100) to a @RequestParam int limit. The constraint is silently ignored. Why?
choices: @Min/@Max only work on field declarations, not parameters.
The controller class is missing @Validated; method-parameter constraints require it. With @Valid + DTOs the class-level annotation isn't needed, but for raw @RequestParam constraints it is.
Primitive int parameters can't be validated; switch to Integer.
Bean Validation 3.0 dropped support for @RequestParam constraints.
questions.explanation: Constraint validation on individual @RequestParam/@PathVariable values requires @Validated on the controller class. @Valid on a @RequestBody DTO works without it. This is a common silent failure for .NET developers expecting DataAnnotations to 'just work'.

questions.question: Why does a typical custom ConstraintValidator return true for null input?
choices: Because Jakarta forbids ConstraintValidator from inspecting null.

By convention: null-handling belongs to @NotNull / @NotBlank. Returning true for null lets you compose validators cleanly (e.g. @NotNull @ValidSku) without each validator re-implementing the null check.
Because the JVM throws NullPointerException on any null-check inside a validator.
Because Spring caches null inputs and bypasses validators.
questions.explanation: The composition convention is: separate null-checking (@NotNull / @NotBlank) from format-checking (@ValidSku, @Email, ...). If every validator rejected null on its own, users couldn't opt into nullable-with-format-when-present semantics. Skipping null also keeps validators single-purpose.
